message:
feat: write-back cache tests, btrfs scrub alignment fix, FUSE readdir bytes, MVCC snapshot lifecycle

ffs-block: Add three new tests for ARC cache write-back policy behavior.
The first validates that the default write policy is WriteThrough. The
second verifies that WriteBack mode defers physical writes until sync()
is called, and that reads against dirty (unflushed) blocks still return
the correct data from cache. The third confirms that when a dirty block
is evicted due to cache capacity pressure, it is automatically flushed
to the backing device rather than silently discarded — preventing data
loss on cache-full scenarios.

ffs-cli: Fix btrfs scrub block size selection to handle images whose
length is not evenly divisible by nodesize. Previously the scrub command
used nodesize directly as the block size, which caused ByteBlockDevice
construction to fail with an alignment error on images whose size is not
a multiple of nodesize (e.g. a 4 KiB-sectorsize image at an odd total
length). The new choose_btrfs_scrub_block_size() function walks powers
of two downward from nodesize to sectorsize (min 4 KiB for superblock
coverage) and picks the largest size that evenly divides the image
length. Also adds a blocks_error_or_higher counter to ScrubOutput using
a BTreeSet-based dedup on block numbers, providing a quick "how many
distinct blocks have Error+ severity" metric in both JSON and text
output. The human-readable report now prints a single summary line
instead of delegating to Display on the raw report, giving consistent
formatting across output modes.

ffs-fuse: Fix readdir to construct entry names from raw bytes via
OsStr::from_bytes on Unix instead of going through name_str() which
performs a lossy UTF-8 conversion. Filenames on Linux are arbitrary byte
sequences (only NUL and '/' are forbidden); the previous path silently
replaced non-UTF-8 bytes with U+FFFD, making files with such names
unreachable via subsequent lookup/stat calls. The cfg(not(unix)) path
retains the lossy conversion as a fallback since OsStr::from_bytes is
Unix-only.

ffs-mvcc: Wire up automatic MVCC snapshot lifecycle management via
register_snapshot in MvccBlockDevice::new() and release_snapshot in a
new Drop impl. Previously, creating an MvccBlockDevice did not inform
the shared MvccStore that a snapshot was actively held, which meant the
store's garbage collection could not safely determine which version
chain entries are still visible to live readers. Without this
registration, old versions could be pruned while a concurrent reader
still references them — a use-after-free in the version chain. The Drop
impl ensures snapshots are always released even on panic unwinds, and
includes a debug_assert to catch double-release bugs during development.
A new test validates the full register/release lifecycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
